# Code of Conduct

## Lucid Technologies Standard

This repository is maintained under a professional collaboration standard focused on:

- respectful, evidence-based technical discussion
- scientific integrity and traceability
- lawful and ethical handling of AI, data, and security concerns
- inclusion of contributors acting in good faith

## Expected Behavior

Contributors are expected to:

- communicate clearly and respectfully
- critique ideas, code, designs, and evidence rather than people
- document assumptions, risks, and limitations honestly
- report security, safety, data, and misuse concerns promptly
- preserve provenance when discussing AI-generated, citizen-contributed, or derived data
- follow repository contribution, security, and governance guidance

## Unacceptable Behavior

The following are not acceptable in this project:

- harassment, discrimination, intimidation, or personal attacks
- intentional falsification of results, provenance, test outcomes, or artifact integrity
- publication of sensitive data, private keys, credentials, or restricted participant data
- submission of malicious code, deceptive artifacts, or counterfeit trust material
- use of the project to facilitate unlawful surveillance, fraud, or harmful misuse

## Enforcement

Maintainers may remove content, restrict participation, or reject contributions that conflict with this code of conduct, the repository security policy, or the project governance standards.

## Reporting

For conduct or governance concerns, open a private maintainer contact through the security reporting path described in [`SECURITY.md`](SECURITY.md) when public discussion would be inappropriate.
